nodes.name: SecretList.tsx
nodes.summary: React component for displaying and managing a list of workspace or project secrets, likely providing CRUD interactions for secret entries.

nodes.name: SettingsModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal dialog component for presenting settings panels, used to configure workspace, project, or company-level settings within a dialog overlay.

nodes.name: WorkspaceRuntimeControls.tsx
nodes.summary: Exports reusable UI components and builder utilities for controlling workspace runtime services and jobs — start, stop, restart, and run actions with pending-state management.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports six symbols including helper builder functions and two React components (WorkspaceRuntimeControls and WorkspaceRuntimeQuickControls) for flexible composition.

nodes.name: hasRunningRuntimeServices
nodes.summary: Checks whether any runtime services in the list are in a running state.

nodes.name: buildWorkspaceRuntimeControlSections
nodes.summary: Builds structured sections (services, jobs, otherServices) for the runtime controls UI by matching workspace command definitions to running runtime services.

nodes.name: buildWorkspaceRuntimeControlItems
nodes.summary: Flattens workspace runtime control sections into a single array of control items for rendering.

nodes.name: getRunningRuntimeServiceUrl
nodes.summary: Retrieves the URL of the first running runtime service from the sections data structure.

nodes.name: WorkspaceRuntimeControls
nodes.summary: Main exported React component rendering runtime service and job control panels with start/stop/restart actions, empty states, and disabled hints.

nodes.name: WorkspaceRuntimeQuickControls
nodes.summary: Compact variant of the runtime controls component showing quick action buttons with optional service URL linking.

nodes.name: useAgentMemberships.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook for fetching and managing agent membership data within a company or project context.

nodes.name: useIssueList.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that fetches and manages a paginated or filtered list of issues/tasks, providing state for issue browsing components.

nodes.name: useProjectMemberships.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook for querying project membership data, exposing member lists for project-level permission and display logic.

nodes.name: useRoutines.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook for fetching and managing routine (scheduled task) data, used by routine-related UI components.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that sets up routing structure, authentication guards, and the top-level page layout for the Paperclip UI.

nodes.name: CliAuth.tsx
nodes.summary: Page component for CLI OAuth challenge approval — fetches the pending challenge via query params, allows users to approve or cancel CLI authentication from the browser.

nodes.name: CliAuthPage
nodes.summary: Manages the full CLI auth challenge flow: fetches session and challenge state, handles approve/cancel mutations, and renders the appropriate UI for each auth state.

nodes.name: CompanyPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary page component for the company workspace, orchestrating navigation and rendering of company-level views including agents, projects, goals, issues, and settings.

nodes.name: InvitePage.tsx
nodes.summary: Page component handling the company invitation flow, allowing invited users to accept an invitation and join a Paperclip company workspace.

nodes.name: ProjectPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Project-scoped page component that renders project details, issues, workspaces, and settings within the company context.

nodes.name: SignInPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Authentication page that handles user sign-in via email magic link or OAuth providers, coordinating with the auth API.

nodes.name: VerifyPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Short page component for handling the email verification step after a sign-in magic link is clicked.

nodes.name: store.ts
nodes.summary: Global client-side state store for the UI, managing application-wide state such as selected company, navigation context, and UI preferences.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: General-purpose utility functions for the UI layer, providing helpers for formatting, string manipulation, and common UI logic.

nodes.name: export-db.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that exports the Paperclip PostgreSQL database to a dump file, used for backup and data migration workflows.

nodes.name: paperclip-issue-update.sh
nodes.summary: CLI utility script that PATCHes a Paperclip issue status and/or comment via the REST API, accepting piped markdown content from stdin and supporting dry-run mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jq for JSON construction and curl for API calls; reads multiline comment from stdin to preserve newlines.

nodes.name: run-docker-postgres.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that spins up a local PostgreSQL Docker container with Paperclip-specific configuration for local development.

nodes.name: send-slack-alert.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that posts an alert message to a configured Slack webhook, used by CI/CD pipelines and automation scripts for notifications.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose configuration defining a two-service stack: a PostgreSQL database and the Paperclip server built from the root Dockerfile, with persistent volumes and environment-based configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a health-check dependency so the server only starts after postgres is ready.

nodes.name: nginx.conf
nodes.summary: Nginx reverse proxy configuration for routing HTTP traffic to the Paperclip server, likely handling static asset serving and API proxying.

nodes.name: paperclip-startup.sh
nodes.summary: Container entrypoint startup script that runs database migrations and then starts the Paperclip server process inside the Docker container.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm monorepo workspace configuration listing all package directories including server, ui, cli, and plugin packages, with exclusions for standalone plugin examples.

nodes.name: vitest.workspace.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest workspace configuration that coordinates test suites across multiple monorepo packages, defining which packages participate in the workspace test run.
